title: Standard outputs should be avoided directly to log anything
description:
In software development, logs serve as a record of events within an application, providing crucial insights for debugging.  When logging, it is essential to ensure that the logs are:
uniformly formatted for readability
securely logged when dealing with sensitive data
Those requirements are not met if a program directly writes to the standard outputs (e. g. , console).
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Avoid direct console output - use a proper logging library instead
